content: COVID-19 is caused by SARS-CoV-2, a betacoronavirus with spike protein mediating ACE2 receptor entry. Clinical spectrum ranges from asymptomatic infection to severe pneumonia with ARDS, multiorgan failure, and death. Long COVID (post-acute sequelae of SARS-CoV-2) involves symptoms persisting ≥3 months after acute infection: fatigue, dyspnea, cognitive dysfunction ('brain fog'), post-exertional malaise, autonomic dysfunction (POTS). Pathophysiology includes viral persistence, immune dysregulation, endothelial dysfunction, and microclots. Variants of concern have evolved: Alpha, Delta, and predominantly Omicron lineages (BA.1, BA.2, BA.4/5, XBB, JN.1) with increased transmissibility and immune escape but generally reduced severity in vaccinated populations.
